must_fix: Blocking: apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts uses Promise.race timeout around fetch without aborting the Bot API request, then records telegram_api_timeout and schedules retry; packages/db/src/telegram-egress.ts later reclaims retry_scheduled/stale sending rows and apps/core/src/runtime/runtime-lifecycle.ts replays them through the gateway. A timed-out or crashed send can still be delivered by Telegram but remain non-terminal in the outbox, so the retry worker can send the same action_id again. This violates AC-F0029-13 and the duplicate-prevention NFR. Fix by making unknown transport outcomes non-resendable until reconciled, or by adding a real cancellable transport/claim protocol whose tests prove one visible Bot API send per action_id across timeout, crash-after-send, and stale-sending recovery.

Blocking: apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts sends parameters.text after recordIntent even when recordIntent returned an existing row for the same action_id. A duplicate/concurrent invocation with different parameters can acquire the pending/retry claim and send text that does not match the durable outbox intent, breaking the durable evidence/idempotency contract in AC-F0029-11/12/18. Fix by treating the stored outbox row as authoritative after recordIntent conflict, or rejecting conflicting payloads before any send claim.
evidence: Reviewed current uncommitted F-0029 implementation with focus on packages/db/src/telegram-egress.ts send claim/listReadyToRetry, apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts telegram.sendMessage transport path, and apps/core/src/runtime/runtime-lifecycle.ts retry worker.
Normative source: docs/ssot/features/F-0029-operator-only-telegram-conversational-egress-reply-loop.md AC-F0029-11 through AC-F0029-18 and duplicate-prevention NFR.

notes: FAIL: send-claim exclusivity improved, but stale/timeout recovery can still produce duplicate visible Telegram messages and conflicting durable intent evidence.
